title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA expects nearly 1 million attendees for the St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston on March 15.
Red Line to operate on rush hour schedule; Broadway Station may be bypassed due to heavy crowds.
Temporary detours and shuttle services planned; bus routes 9 and 11 to suspend service on parade day.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached a record 81.3% in 2025, increasing by 1.6% from 2024.
Despite improvements, Boston's graduation rate lags 8 points behind the Massachusetts state average of 89.3%.
English learners and students with disabilities saw significant gains, with nearly 5% and over 5% increases, respectively.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have an 11-14-5 record on the road this season, which may hinder their playoff success.
Massive road struggles have contributed to the Bruins' second wild-card position, with 35-22-6 overall and 76 points.
Of their last 21 games, 12 are on the road with crucial matches against playoff contenders and the Blue Jackets.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support pediatric patients at Spaulding Rehab.
As Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation, Wishart aims to integrate research into care and fund adaptive sports programs.
Wishart emphasizes the need for resources and expertise to provide cutting-edge treatment for children regardless of their backgrounds.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight Massachusetts children have died from flu this season, according to state health data ending February 28.
As of February 28, the Massachusetts flu season saw 298 adult deaths and moderate flu-like activity in Boston.
Experts continue to recommend flu vaccinations for everyone over six months as only 38.1% of Massachusetts residents are vaccinated.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after backlash from stakeholders, including fans and partners.
Former Celtics center Luke Kornet's open letter questioning the promotion gained widespread attention on social media.
The event intended to highlight Atlanta's Magic City with chicken wings and a performance by hip-hop artist T.I.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O’Neal was unaware of Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman until viewing highlights of a game against the Cavaliers.
Baylor Scheierman, a 2024 first-round pick for the Celtics, has played 59 games this season, starting 16 during Jayson Tatum's absence.
Scheierman averages 4.8 points and 3.2 rebounds per game and is shooting 38.9% from three-point range this season.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cease coordinating deportation flights for ICE at Hanscom Field, Bedford.
Healey's letter accuses ICE of due process violations under Trump's mass deportation campaign, calls Signature a key partner.
132 ICE flights departed from Hanscom during Trump's second term's first year, with 13 in January 2026, per advocacy data.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a metal grate at Boston's Omni Parker House, sustaining injuries.
Boston Inspectional Services cited the hotel for a building code violation due to rotted grate brackets.
The hotel must hire a structural engineer to inspect and report on all vent grates' integrity.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to entertain customers at the pumps.
A retired violist will soon start performing, but Kertzman is still looking for more musicians.
The musical performances will occur only in warm weather to protect the viola from the cold.
